codeReview.summary: The commit adds 761 lines across 3 new test files (server.quote-compare.test.ts, curve.test.ts, observability.test.ts) plus lockfile normalization. All test files are well-structured with proper mock isolation, realistic HTTP server lifecycle, and comprehensive branch coverage. The tests correctly verify /quote and /compare endpoint behavior (success, fallback, error, validation, feature flags), Curve module initialization/quoting/error handling, and all observability modules (analytics, error-insights, metrics, tracing, sentry). No new dependencies were added—the package-lock.json changes are just peer dependency markers. The interactive verification via browser automation confirmed all 6 expected behaviors: autocomplete flow, endpoint formats, URL param updates, zero console errors, coverage threshold met (91.96% statements), and wallet sender propagation in auto-refresh.

issues.description: FALLBACK_ACCOUNT is constructed via string concatenation to match the server constant. While it works (verified: concatenation yields 0xEe7aE85f2Fe2239E27D9c1E23fFFe168D63b4055 matching server.ts:76), a comment explaining why it's split would prevent future confusion. This is purely cosmetic.

The analytics max-cap stress test pushes 10005 extra events after an initial 2, expecting capped.totalQuotes === 10000. This is correct (splice trims to MAX_EVENTS on each push), but the tight loop of 10005 iterations may slow test execution slightly. Not a correctness issue.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker created test files following the naming pattern src/__tests__/<module-area>.test.ts (server.quote-compare.test.ts, curve.test.ts, observability.test.ts), which aligns with AGENTS.md conventions. No violations found.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All new files in src/__tests__/*.test.ts; AGENTS.md says 'Test files: src/__tests__/*.test.ts'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the fullstack-worker skill procedure faithfully: read feature description, wrote tests, verified with typecheck/lint/test, did interactive browser checks, and produced a complete handoff. The skill's 'Write Tests First (Red Phase)' step was effectively adapted—since this is a verification feature, the worker wrote tests to fill coverage gaps rather than doing strict red-green-refactor, which is appropriate for an integration-verification task.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true with no deviations; verification.commandsRun shows all 3 required checks passed; interactiveChecks has 5 entries covering full flow

sharedStateObservations.observation: The user-testing.md library file notes that 'ERC-6963 detection and wallet signing cannot be automated without a wallet extension', but the worker successfully automated ERC-6963 wallet testing by injecting a mock provider via browser evaluate. This technique (injecting a mock ERC-6963 provider) could be documented as a testing pattern for future wallet-related features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff interactiveChecks shows: 'Injected a mock ERC-6963 wallet provider via agent-browser eval, connected via Connect Wallet flow' — proving the user-testing.md limitation is partially incorrect/outdated

summary: Integration verification feature passes review. The worker added 3 comprehensive test files (761 new lines) covering /quote and /compare endpoints, Curve module, and all observability modules. Coverage now exceeds the 80% threshold at 91.96% statements. Interactive browser verification confirmed all 6 expected behaviors including wallet sender propagation in auto-refresh. No blocking issues found. Two minor non-blocking observations: a cosmetic string concatenation pattern for the fallback account constant and a stress-test loop that could be slightly more efficient. The worker also demonstrated a useful testing technique (mock ERC-6963 provider injection) that could be documented in the knowledge library.
